heading: Qualifying advanced energy project credit
label: 48C Qualifying advanced energy project credit

Effective DateSection applicable to periods after , under rules similar to the rules of as in effect on the day before , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2009 Amendment note under .Feb. 17, 2009Nov. 5, 1990section 48(m) of this titlesection 1302(d) of Pub. L. 111–5section 46 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: For purposes of section 46, the qualifying advanced energy project credit for any taxable year is an amount equal to 30 percent of the qualified investment for such taxable year with respect to any qualifying advanced energy project of the taxpayer.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a), the qualified investment for any taxable year is the basis of eligible property placed in service by the taxpayer during such taxable year which is part of a qualifying advanced energy project.

children.heading: Certain qualified progress expenditures rules made applicable
children.label: 2 Certain qualified progress expenditures rules made applicable
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of subsections (c)(4) and (d) of section 46 (as in effect on the day before the enactment of the Revenue Reconciliation Act of 1990) shall apply for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 3 Limitation
children.text: The amount which is treated as the qualified investment for all taxable years with respect to any qualifying advanced energy project shall not exceed the amount designated by the Secretary as eligible for the credit under this section.

children.label: I
children.text: property designed to be used to produce energy from the sun, water, wind, geothermal deposits (within the meaning of section 613(e)(2)), or other renewable resources,

children.label: II
children.text: fuel cells, microturbines, or energy storage systems and components,

children.label: III
children.text: electric grid modernization equipment or components,

children.label: IV
children.text: property designed to capture, remove, use, or sequester carbon oxide emissions,

children.label: aa
children.text: renewable, or

children.label: bb
children.text: low-carbon and low-emission,

children.label: VI
children.text: property designed to produce energy conservation technologies (including residential, commercial, and industrial applications),

children.label: aa
children.text: technologies, components, or materials for such vehicles, and

children.label: bb
children.text: associated charging or refueling infrastructure,

children.label: VIII
children.text: hybrid vehicles with a gross vehicle weight rating of not less than 14,000 pounds, as well as technologies, components, or materials for such vehicles, or

children.label: IX
children.text: other advanced energy property designed to reduce greenhouse gas emissions as may be determined by the Secretary,

children.label: I
children.text: low- or zero-carbon process heat systems,

children.label: II
children.text: carbon capture, transport, utilization and storage systems,

children.label: III
children.text: energy efficiency and reduction in waste from industrial processes, or

children.label: IV
children.text: any other industrial technology designed to reduce greenhouse gas emissions, as determined by the Secretary, or

children.label: iii
children.text: which re-equips, expands, or establishes an industrial facility for the processing, refining, or recycling of critical materials (as defined in section 7002(a) of the Energy Act of 2020 () .30 U.S.C. 1606(a)1So in original. Probably should be followed by a closing parenthesis.1

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: Such term shall not include any portion of a project for the production of any property which is used in the refining or blending of any transportation fuel (other than renewable fuels).

children.label: i
children.text: the production or recycling of property described in clause (i) of paragraph (1)(A),

children.label: ii
children.text: re-equipping an industrial or manufacturing facility described in clause (ii) of such paragraph, or

children.label: iii
children.text: re-equipping, expanding, or establishing an industrial facility described in clause (iii) of such paragraph,

children.label: i
children.text: tangible personal property, or

children.label: ii
children.text: other tangible property (not including a building or its structural components), but only if such property is used as an integral part of the qualified investment credit facility, and

children.label: C
children.text: with respect to which depreciation (or amortization in lieu of depreciation) is allowable.

children.heading: Qualifying advanced energy project program
children.label: d Qualifying advanced energy project program

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this section, the Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of Energy, shall establish a qualifying advanced energy project program to consider and award certifications for qualified investments eligible for credits under this section to qualifying advanced energy project sponsors.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: The total amount of credits that may be allocated under the program shall not exceed $2,300,000,000.

children.heading: Application period
children.label: A Application period
children.text: Each applicant for certification under this paragraph shall submit an application containing such information as the Secretary may require during the 2-year period beginning on the date the Secretary establishes the program under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Time to meet criteria for certification
children.label: B Time to meet criteria for certification
children.text: Each applicant for certification shall have 1 year from the date of acceptance by the Secretary of the application during which to provide to the Secretary evidence that the requirements of the certification have been met.

children.heading: Period of issuance
children.label: C Period of issuance
children.text: An applicant which receives a certification shall have 3 years from the date of issuance of the certification in order to place the project in service and if such project is not placed in service by that time period, then the certification shall no longer be valid.

children.label: A
children.text: shall take into consideration only those projects where there is a reasonable expectation of commercial viability, and

children.label: i
children.text: will provide the greatest domestic job creation (both direct and indirect) during the credit period,

children.label: ii
children.text: will provide the greatest net impact in avoiding or reducing air pollutants or anthropogenic emissions of greenhouse gases,

children.label: iii
children.text: have the greatest potential for technological innovation and commercial deployment,

children.label: iv
children.text: have the lowest levelized cost of generated or stored energy, or of measured reduction in energy consumption or greenhouse gas emission (based on costs of the full supply chain), and

children.label: v
children.text: have the shortest project time from certification to completion.

children.heading: Review
children.label: A Review
children.text: Not later than 4 years after the date of enactment of this section, the Secretary shall review the credits allocated under this section as of such date.

children.label: i
children.text: there is an insufficient quantity of qualifying applications for certification pending at the time of the review, or

children.label: ii
children.text: any certification made pursuant to paragraph (2) has been revoked pursuant to paragraph (2)(B) because the project subject to the certification has been delayed as a result of third party opposition or litigation to the proposed project.

children.heading: Reallocation
children.label: C Reallocation
children.text: If the Secretary determines that credits under this section are available for reallocation pursuant to the requirements set forth in paragraph (2), the Secretary is authorized to conduct an additional program for applications for certification.

children.heading: Disclosure of allocations
children.label: 5 Disclosure of allocations
children.text: The Secretary shall, upon making a certification under this subsection, publicly disclose the identity of the applicant and the amount of the credit with respect to such applicant.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this subsection, the Secretary shall establish a program to consider and award certifications for qualified investments eligible for credits under this section to qualifying advanced energy project sponsors.

children.label: A
children.text: is described in clause (iii) of section 45(b)(11)(B), and

children.label: B
children.text: prior to the date of enactment of this subsection, had no project which received a certification and allocation of credits under subsection (d).

children.heading: Application requirement
children.label: A Application requirement
children.text: Each applicant for certification under this subsection shall submit an application at such time and containing such information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Time to meet criteria for certification
children.label: B Time to meet criteria for certification
children.text: Each applicant for certification shall have 2 years from the date of acceptance by the Secretary of the application during which to provide to the Secretary evidence that the requirements of the certification have been met.

children.heading: Period of issuance
children.label: C Period of issuance
children.text: An applicant which receives a certification shall have 2 years from the date of issuance of the certification in order to place the project in service and to notify the Secretary that such project has been so placed in service, and if such project is not placed in service by that time period, then the certification shall no longer be valid. If any certification is revoked under this subparagraph, the amount of the limitation under paragraph (2) shall not be increased by the amount of the credit with respect to such revoked certification.

children.heading: Location of project
children.label: D Location of project
children.text: In the case of an applicant which receives a certification, if the Secretary determines that the project has been placed in service at a location which is materially different than the location specified in the application for such project, the certification shall no longer be valid.

children.heading: Credit rate conditioned upon wage and apprenticeship requirements
children.label: 4 Credit rate conditioned upon wage and apprenticeship requirements

children.heading: Base rate
children.label: A Base rate
children.text: For purposes of allocations under this subsection, the amount of the credit determined under subsection (a) shall be determined by substituting “6 percent” for “30 percent”.

children.heading: Alternative rate
children.label: B Alternative rate
children.text: In the case of any project which satisfies the requirements of paragraphs (5)(A) and (6), subparagraph (A) shall not apply.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The requirements described in this subparagraph with respect to a project are that the taxpayer shall ensure that any laborers and mechanics employed by the taxpayer or any contractor or subcontractor in the re-equipping, expansion, or establishment of a manufacturing facility shall be paid wages at rates not less than the prevailing rates for construction, alteration, or repair of a similar character in the locality in which such project is located as most recently determined by the Secretary of Labor, in accordance with subchapter IV of chapter 31 of title 40, United States Code.

children.heading: Correction and penalty related to failure to satisfy wage requirements
children.label: B Correction and penalty related to failure to satisfy wage requirements
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 45(b)(7)(B) shall apply.

children.heading: Apprenticeship requirements
children.label: 6 Apprenticeship requirements
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 45(b)(8) shall apply.

children.heading: Disclosure of allocations
children.label: 7 Disclosure of allocations
children.text: The Secretary shall, upon making a certification under this subsection, publicly disclose the identity of the applicant and the amount of the credit with respect to such applicant.

children.heading: Denial of double benefit
children.label: f Denial of double benefit
children.text: A credit shall not be allowed under this section for any qualified investment for which a credit is allowed under section 48, 48A, 48B, 48E, 45Q, or 45V.
